treatment_information.specific_treatment: left superficial temporal artery-to-middle cerebral artery (STA-MCA) bypass and ligation of the left extracranial internal carotid artery (EICA)
treatment_information.treatment_response: No neurologic abnormalities following the ligation; validity of the left STA-MCA bypass was indicated by postoperative CTA and DSA.
Hematoma disappeared; muscle strength in the left limbs returned to 4 grades.
Patient was discharged with no neurologic abnormalities 10 days after the right STA-MCA bypass.

treatment_timeline.event: Patient admitted to emergency department and underwent emergent tracheal intubation for hoarseness and breathing difficulty.
Imaging examinations revealed left EICA aneurysm and moyamoya disease (MMD).
Patient underwent left STA-MCA bypass and ligation of the left EICA simultaneously.
Patient suffered deteriorated muscle strength from 5 to 2 grades in the left limbs.
Emergency CT showed hemorrhagic stroke in the right basal ganglia region.

treatment_timeline.date: 20 days after onset of hemorrhagic stroke
treatment_timeline.event: Hematoma disappeared; muscle strength in the left limbs returned to 4 grades.
Right STA-MCA bypass was carried out.
Patient was discharged with no neurologic abnormalities.
Patient lived a normal life with no neurologic abnormalities; imaging examinations indicated efficacy of bilateral STA-MCA bypass and disappearance of left EICA aneurysm.

patient_outcome.final_status: No neurologic abnormalities; lived a normal life.
patient_outcome.complications: Hemorrhagic stroke in the right basal ganglia region 20 days after left STA-MCA bypass.
patient_outcome.recovery_timeline: Hematoma disappeared 20 days after onset of hemorrhagic stroke; muscle strength returned to 4 grades.
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: Lived a normal life with no neurologic abnormalities.
